entries.title: Document name and identification

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: This certificate policy (CP) contains the requirements for the issuance and management of publicly-trusted TLS Server certificates, as adopted by the CA/Browser Forum.

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: The following Certificate Policy identifiers are reserved for use by CAs to assert compliance with this document (OID arc 2.23.140.1.2) as follows:

entries.title: PKI Participants
entries.snippet: PKI Participants

entries.title: PKI Participants
entries.snippet: The CA/Browser Forum is a voluntary organization of Certification Authorities and suppliers of Internet browser and other relying-party software applications.

entries.title: Certification Authorities
entries.snippet: Certification Authorities

entries.title: Certification Authorities
entries.snippet: Certification Authority (CA) is defined in [Section 1.6](#16-definitions-and-acronyms). Current CA Members of the CA/Browser Forum are listed here: <https://cabforum.org/members>.

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: Registration Authorities

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: With the exception of [Section 3.2.2.4](#3224-validation-of-domain-authorization-or-control), [Section 3.2.2.5](#3225-authentication-for-an-ip-address) and (effective 2026-03-15) [Section 3.2.2.8](#32

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: Before the CA authorizes a Delegated Third Party to perform a delegated function, the CA SHALL contractually require the Delegated Third Party to:

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: Meet the qualification requirements of [Section 5.3.1](#531-qualifications-experience-and-clearance-requirements), when applicable to the delegated function;

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: Retain documentation in accordance with [Section 5.5.2](#552-retention-period-for-archive);

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: Abide by the other provisions of these Requirements that are applicable to the delegated function; and

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: Comply with

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: The CA MAY designate an Enterprise RA to verify certificate requests from the Enterprise RA's own organization.

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL NOT accept certificate requests authorized by an Enterprise RA unless the following requirements are satisfied:

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL confirm that the requested Fully-Qualified Domain Name(s) are within the Enterprise RA's verified Domain Namespace.

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: If the certificate request includes a Subject name of a type other than a Fully-Qualified Domain Name, the CA SHALL confirm that the name is either that of the delegated enterprise, or an Affiliate of

entries.title: Registration Authorities
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL impose these limitations as a contractual requirement on the Enterprise RA and monitor compliance by the Enterprise RA.

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Applicant**: The natural person or Legal Entity that applies for (or seeks renewal of) a Certificate. Once the Certificate is issued, the Applicant is referred to as the Subscriber. For Certificates

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Applicant Representative**: A natural person or human sponsor who is either the Applicant, employed by the Applicant, or an authorized agent who has express authority to represent the Applicant:

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Application Software Supplier**: A supplier of Internet browser software or other relying-party application software that displays or uses Certificates and incorporates Root Certificates.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Attestation Letter**: A letter attesting that Subject Information is correct written by an accountant, lawyer, government official, or other reliable third party customarily relied upon for such inf

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Audit Report**: A report from a Qualified Auditor stating the Qualified Auditor's opinion on whether an entity's processes and controls comply with the mandatory provisions of these Requirements.

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Authorized Ports**: One of the following ports: 80 (http), 443 (https), 25 (smtp), 22 (ssh).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Base Domain Name**: The portion of an applied-for FQDN that is the first Domain Name node left of a registry-controlled or public suffix plus the registry-controlled or public suffix (e.g. "example.

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **CA Key Pair**: A Key Pair where the Public Key appears as the Subject Public Key Info in one or more Root CA Certificate(s) and/or Subordinate CA Certificate(s).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate**: An electronic document that uses a digital signature to bind a public key and an identity.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Data**: Certificate requests and data related thereto (whether obtained from the Applicant or otherwise) in the CA's possession or control or to which the CA has access.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Management Process**: Processes, practices, and procedures associated with the use of keys, software, and hardware, by which the CA verifies Certificate Data, issues Certificates, mainta

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Policy**: A set of rules that indicates the applicability of a named Certificate to a particular community and/or PKI implementation with common security requirements.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Problem Report**: Complaint of suspected Key Compromise, Certificate misuse, or other types of fraud, compromise, misuse, or inappropriate conduct related to Certificates.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Profile**: A set of documents or files that defines Certificate content and Certificate extensions, e.g. a Section in a CA's CPS or a certificate template file used by CA software.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Revocation List**: A regularly updated time-stamped list of revoked Certificates that is created and digitally signed by the CA that issued the Certificates.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certification Authority**: An organization that is responsible for the creation, issuance, revocation, and management of Certificates. The term applies equally to both Root CAs and Subordinate CAs.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certification Practice Statement**: One of several documents forming the governance framework in which Certificates are created, issued, managed, and used.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Control**: "Control" (and its correlative meanings, "controlled by" and "under common control with") means possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to: (1) direct the management, personnel,

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Country**: Either a member of the United Nations OR a geographic region recognized as a Sovereign State by at least two UN member nations.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Certificate**: A certificate that is used to establish a trust relationship between two CAs.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **CSPRNG**: A random number generator intended for use in a cryptographic system.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Delegated Third Party**: A natural person or Legal Entity that is not the CA but is authorized by the CA, and whose activities are not within the scope of the appropriate CA audits, to assist in the

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **DNS CAA Email Contact**: The email address defined in [Appendix A.1.1](#a11-caa-contactemail-property).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **DNS CAA Phone Contact**: The phone number defined in [Appendix A.1.2](#a12-caa-contactphone-property).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **DNS TXT Record Email Contact**: The email address defined in [Appendix A.2.1](#a21-dns-txt-record-email-contact).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **DNS TXT Record Phone Contact**: The phone number defined in [Appendix A.2.2](#a22-dns-txt-record-phone-contact).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Domain Contact**: The Domain Name Registrant, technical contact, or administrative contact (or the equivalent under a ccTLD) as listed in the WHOIS record of the Base Domain Name or in a DNS SOA rec

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Domain Label**: From [RFC 8499](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8499): "An ordered list of zero or more octets that makes up a portion of a domain name. Using graph theory, a label identifi

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Domain Name**: An ordered list of one or more Domain Labels assigned to a node in the Domain Name System.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Domain Namespace**: The set of all possible Domain Names that are subordinate to a single node in the Domain Name System.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Domain Name Registrant**: Sometimes referred to as the "owner" of a Domain Name, but more properly the person(s) or entity(ies) registered with a Domain Name Registrar as having the right to control

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Domain Name Registrar**: A person or entity that registers Domain Names under the auspices of or by agreement with:

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: i. the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN), ii. a national Domain Name authority/registry, or iii. a Network Information Center (including their affiliates, contractors, delega

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Enterprise RA**: An employee or agent of an organization unaffiliated with the CA who authorizes issuance of Certificates to that organization.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Expiry Date**: The "Not After" date in a Certificate that defines the end of a Certificate's validity period.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Fully-Qualified Domain Name**: A Domain Name that includes the Domain Labels of all superior nodes in the Internet Domain Name System.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Government Entity**: A government-operated legal entity, agency, department, ministry, branch, or similar element of the government of a country, or political subdivision within such country (such a

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **High Risk Certificate Request**: A Request that the CA flags for additional scrutiny by reference to internal criteria and databases maintained by the CA, which may include names at higher risk for

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Internal Name**: A string of characters (not an IP address) in a Common Name or Subject Alternative Name field of a Certificate that cannot be verified as globally unique within the public DNS at th

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **IP Address**: A 32-bit or 128-bit number assigned to a device that uses the Internet Protocol for communication.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **IP Address Contact**: The person(s) or entity(ies) registered with an IP Address Registration Authority as having the right to control how one or more IP Addresses are used.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **IP Address Registration Authority**: The Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) or a Regional Internet Registry (RIPE, APNIC, ARIN, AfriNIC, LACNIC).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **IP Reverse Zone Suffix**: One of the two FQDNs that consist of the Domain Labels "in-addr.arpa" or "ip6.arpa". These two FQDNs serve as the root of the IP version 4 and IP version 6 reverse mapping

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Issuing CA**: In relation to a particular Certificate, the CA that issued the Certificate. This could be either a Root CA or a Subordinate CA.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Key Compromise**: A Private Key is said to be compromised if its value has been disclosed to an unauthorized person, or an unauthorized person has had access to it.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Key Generation Script**: A documented plan of procedures for the generation of a CA Key Pair.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Key Pair**: The Private Key and its associated Public Key.

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Legal Entity**: An association, corporation, partnership, proprietorship, trust, government entity or other entity with legal standing in a country's legal system.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Linting**: A process in which the content of digitally signed data such as a Precertificate [RFC 6962](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6962), Certificate, Certificate Revocation List, or OC

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Multi-Perspective Issuance Corroboration**: A process by which the determinations made during domain validation and CAA checking by the Primary Network Perspective are corroborated by other Network

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Network Perspective**: Related to Multi-Perspective Issuance Corroboration. A system (e.g., a cloud-hosted server instance) or collection of network components (e.g., a VPN and corresponding infrast

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Non-Reserved LDH Label**: From [RFC 5890](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5890): "The set of valid LDH labels that do not have '`--`' in the third and fourth positions."

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Object Identifier**: A unique alphanumeric or numeric identifier registered under the International Organization for Standardization's applicable standard for a specific object or object class.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **OCSP Responder**: An online server operated under the authority of the CA and connected to its Repository for processing Certificate status requests. See also, Online Certificate Status Protocol.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Onion Domain Name**: A Fully Qualified Domain Name ending with the [RFC 7686](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7686) ".onion" Special-Use Domain Name. For example, `2gzyxa5ihm7nsggfxnu52rck2

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Online Certificate Status Protocol**: An online Certificate-checking protocol that enables relying-party application software to determine the status of an identified Certificate. See also OCSP Resp

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Parent Company**: A company that Controls a Subsidiary Company.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Pending Prohibition**: The use of a behavior described with this label is highly discouraged, as it is planned to be deprecated and will likely be designated as MUST NOT in the future.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Persistent DCV TXT Record**: A DNS TXT record identifying an Applicant in accordance with [Section 3.2.2.4.22](#322422-dns-txt-record-with-persistent-value).

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Primary Network Perspective**: The Network Perspective used by the CA to make the determination of 1) the CA's authority to issue a Certificate for the requested domain(s) or IP address(es) and 2) t

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Private Key**: The key of a Key Pair that is kept secret by the holder of the Key Pair, and that is used to create Digital Signatures and/or to decrypt electronic records or files that were encrypte

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Public Key**: The key of a Key Pair that may be publicly disclosed by the holder of the corresponding Private Key and that is used by a Relying Party to verify Digital Signatures created with the ho

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Public Key Infrastructure**: A set of hardware, software, people, procedures, rules, policies, and obligations used to facilitate the trustworthy creation, issuance, management, and use of Certifica

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Publicly-Trusted Certificate**: A Certificate that is trusted by virtue of the fact that its corresponding Root Certificate is distributed as a trust anchor in widely-available application software.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **P-Label**: A XN-Label that contains valid output of the Punycode algorithm (as defined in [RFC 3492, Section 6.3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3492#section-6.3)) from the fifth and subse

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Qualified Auditor**: A natural person or Legal Entity that meets the requirements of [Section 8.2](#82-identityqualifications-of-assessor).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Random Value**: A value specified by a CA to the Applicant that exhibits at least 112 bits of entropy.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Registered Domain Name**: A Domain Name that has been registered with a Domain Name Registrar.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Registration Authority (RA)**: Any Legal Entity that is responsible for identification and authentication of subjects of Certificates, but is not a CA, and hence does not sign or issue Certificates.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Reliable Data Source**: An identification document or source of data used to verify Subject Identity Information that is generally recognized among commercial enterprises and governments as reliable

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Reliable Method of Communication**: A method of communication, such as a postal/courier delivery address, telephone number, or email address, that was verified using a source other than the Applican

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Relying Party**: Any natural person or Legal Entity that relies on a Valid Certificate. An Application Software Supplier is not considered a Relying Party when software distributed by such Supplier

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Repository**: An online database containing publicly-disclosed PKI governance documents (such as Certificate Policies and Certification Practice Statements) and Certificate status information, eithe

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Request Token**: A value, derived in a method specified by the CA which binds this demonstration of control to the certificate request. The CA SHOULD define within its CPS (or a document clearly ref

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: The Request Token SHALL incorporate the key used in the certificate request.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: A Request Token MAY include a timestamp to indicate when it was created.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: A Request Token MAY include other information to ensure its uniqueness.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: A Request Token that includes a timestamp SHALL remain valid for no more than 30 days from the time of creation.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: A Request Token that includes a timestamp SHALL be treated as invalid if its timestamp is in the future.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: A Request Token that does not include a timestamp is valid for a single use and the CA SHALL NOT re-use it for a subsequent validation.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: The binding SHALL use a digital signature algorithm or a cryptographic hash algorithm at least as strong as that to be used in signing the certificate request.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Note**: Examples of Request Tokens include, but are not limited to:

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: i. a hash of the public key; or ii. a hash of the Subject Public Key Info [X.509]; or iii. a hash of a PKCS#10 CSR.

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Required Website Content**: Either a Random Value or a Request Token, together with additional information that uniquely identifies the Subscriber, as specified by the CA.

entries.snippet: **Requirements**: The Baseline Requirements found in this document.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Root CA**: The top level Certification Authority whose Root Certificate is distributed by Application Software Suppliers and that issues Subordinate CA Certificates.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Root Certificate**: The self-signed Certificate issued by the Root CA to identify itself and to facilitate verification of Certificates issued to its Subordinate CAs.

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Sovereign State**: A state or country that administers its own government, and is not dependent upon, or subject to, another power.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Subject**: The natural person, device, system, unit, or Legal Entity identified in a Certificate as the Subject. The Subject is either the Subscriber or a device under the control and operation of t

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Subject Identity Information**: Information that identifies the Certificate Subject. Subject Identity Information does not include a Domain Name or an IP Address listed in the `subjectAltName` exten

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Subordinate CA**: A Certification Authority whose Certificate is signed by the Root CA, or another Subordinate CA.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Subscriber**: A natural person or Legal Entity to whom a Certificate is issued and who is legally bound by a Subscriber Agreement or Terms of Use.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Subscriber Agreement**: An agreement between the CA and the Applicant/Subscriber that specifies the rights and responsibilities of the parties.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Subsidiary Company**: A company that is controlled by a Parent Company.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Technically Constrained Subordinate CA Certificate**: A Subordinate CA certificate which uses a combination of Extended Key Usage and/or Name Constraint extensions, as defined within the relevant Ce

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Terms of Use**: Provisions regarding the safekeeping and acceptable uses of a Certificate issued in accordance with these Requirements when the Applicant/Subscriber is an Affiliate of the CA or is t

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Test Certificate**: This term is no longer used in these Baseline Requirements.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Top-Level Domain**: From RFC 8499 (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8499): "A Top-Level Domain is a zone that is one layer below the root, such as "com" or "jp"."

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Trustworthy System**: Computer hardware, software, and procedures that are: reasonably secure from intrusion and misuse; provide a reasonable level of availability, reliability, and correct operatio

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Unregistered Domain Name**: A Domain Name that is not a Registered Domain Name.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Valid Certificate**: A Certificate that passes the validation procedure specified in [RFC 5280](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280).

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Validation Specialist**: Someone who performs the information verification duties specified by these Requirements.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Validity Period**: From [RFC 5280](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280): "The period of time from `notBefore` through `notAfter`, inclusive."

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Wildcard Certificate**: A Certificate containing at least one Wildcard Domain Name in the Subject Alternative Names in the Certificate.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Wildcard Domain Name**: A string starting with "\*." (U+002A ASTERISK, U+002E FULL STOP) immediately followed by a Fully-Qualified Domain Name.

entries.title: Validation of Domain Authorization or Control

entries.title: Validation of Domain Authorization or Control
entries.snippet: When the FQDN is an Onion Domain Name, the CA SHALL validate the FQDN in accordance with Appendix B.

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: Confirm the Applicant's control over the FQDN by:

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: Sending an email to one or more addresses created by using 'admin', 'administrator', 'webmaster', 'hostmaster', or 'postmaster' as the local part, followed by the at-sign ("@"), followed by an Authori

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: including a Random Value in the email; and

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: receiving a confirming response utilizing the Random Value.

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: Each email MAY confirm control of multiple FQDNs, provided the Authorization Domain Name used in the email is an Authorization Domain Name for each FQDN being confirmed.

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: The Random Value SHALL be unique in each email.

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: The email MAY be re-sent in its entirety, including the re-use of the Random Value, provided that its entire contents and recipient SHALL remain unchanged.

entries.title: Email to a Constructed Address
entries.snippet: The Random Value SHALL remain valid for use in a confirming response for no more than 30 days from its creation. The CPS MAY specify a shorter validity period for Random Values.

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The CA MUST confirm the Persistent DCV TXT Record's RDATA value fulfills the following requirements:

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The RDATA value MUST conform to the `issue-value` syntax as defined in [RFC 8659, Section 4.2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8659#section-4.2); and

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The `issuer-domain-name` value MUST be an Issuer Domain Name disclosed by the CA in Section 4.2 of the CA's Certificate Policy and/or Certification Practices Statement; and

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The `issue-value` MUST contain an `accounturi` parameter, where the parameter value is a unique URI (as described by [RFC 8657, Section 3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8657#section-3)) ide

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The `issue-value` MAY contain a `persistUntil` parameter. If present, the parameter value MUST be a base-10 encoded integer representing a UNIX timestamp (the number of seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The `issue-value` MAY contain additional parameters. CAs MUST ignore any unknown parameter keys.

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: If the `persistUntil` parameter is present, the CA MUST evaluate its value. If the time of the check is after the time specified in the `persistUntil` parameter value, the CA MUST NOT use the record a

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: For example, the Persistent DCV TXT Record might look like: `_validation-persist.example.com IN TXT "authority.example; accounturi=https://authority.example/acct/123; persistUntil=1782424856"`

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: The following table shows how the `persistUntil` parameter affects whether a DNS record can be used for validation at different points in time:

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: Table: Examples of how the `persistUntil` parameter affects validation

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: | 2025-06-15T12:00:00Z | 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z (1767225600) | Yes | Validation time is before persistUntil timestamp, so record is usable |

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: | 2025-06-15T12:00:00Z | 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z (1735689600) | No | Validation time is after persistUntil timestamp, so record is not usable |

entries.title: DNS TXT Record with Persistent Value
entries.snippet: | 2025-06-15T12:00:00Z | (not present) | Yes | No persistUntil parameter present, so no time restriction applies |

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: CRLs MUST be available via a publicly-accessible HTTP URL (i.e., "published").

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: Within twenty-four (24) hours of issuing its first Certificate, the CA MUST generate and publish either:

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: a full and complete CRL; OR

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: partitioned (i.e., "sharded") CRLs that, when aggregated, represent the equivalent of a full and complete CRL.

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: CAs issuing Subscriber Certificates:

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: MUST update and publish a new CRL at least every:

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: seven (7) days if all Certificates include an Authority Information Access extension with an `id-ad-ocsp` accessMethod ("AIA OCSP pointer"); or

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: four (4) days in all other cases;

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: MUST update and publish a new CRL within twenty-four (24) hours after recording a Certificate as revoked.

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: CAs issuing CA Certificates:

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: MUST update and publish a new CRL at least every twelve (12) months;

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: MUST update and publish a new CRL within twenty-four (24) hours after recording a Certificate as revoked.

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: CAs MUST continue issuing CRLs until one of the following is true:

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: all Subordinate CA Certificates containing the same Subject Public Key are expired or revoked; OR

entries.title: CRL issuance frequency
entries.snippet: the corresponding Subordinate CA Private Key is destroyed.

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: The CA/Browser Forum's Network and Certificate System Security Requirements are incorporated by reference as if fully set forth herein.

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive security program designed to:

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of Certificate Data and Certificate Management Processes;

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Protect against anticipated threats or hazards to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the Certificate Data and Certificate Management Processes;

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Protect against unauthorized or unlawful access, use, disclosure, alteration, or destruction of any Certificate Data or Certificate Management Processes;

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Protect against accidental loss or destruction of, or damage to, any Certificate Data or Certificate Management Processes; and

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Comply with all other security requirements applicable to the CA by law.

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: The Certificate Management Process MUST include:

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: physical security and environmental controls;

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: system integrity controls, including configuration management, integrity maintenance of trusted code, and malware detection/prevention;

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: network security and firewall management, including port restrictions and IP address filtering;

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: user management, separate trusted-role assignments, education, awareness, and training; and

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: logical access controls, activity logging, and inactivity time-outs to provide individual accountability.

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: The CA's security program MUST include an annual Risk Assessment that:

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Identifies foreseeable internal and external threats that could result in unauthorized access, disclosure, misuse, alteration, or destruction of any Certificate Data or Certificate Management Processe

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Assesses the likelihood and potential damage of these threats, taking into consideration the sensitivity of the Certificate Data and Certificate Management Processes; and

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Assesses the sufficiency of the policies, procedures, information systems, technology, and other arrangements that the CA has in place to counter such threats.

entries.title: MANAGEMENT, OPERATIONAL, AND PHYSICAL CONTROLS
entries.snippet: Based on the Risk Assessment, the CA SHALL develop, implement, and maintain a security plan consisting of security procedures, measures, and products designed to achieve the objectives set forth above

entries.snippet: Recovery time objective (RTO);

entries.snippet: Regular testing of contingency plans.

entries.title: Incident Response and Disaster Recovery Plans
entries.snippet: The CA's plan to maintain or restore the CA's business operations in a timely manner following interruption to or failure of critical business processes

entries.title: Incident Response and Disaster Recovery Plans
entries.snippet: A requirement to store critical cryptographic materials (i.e., secure cryptographic device and activation materials) at an alternate location;

entries.title: Incident Response and Disaster Recovery Plans
entries.snippet: What constitutes an acceptable system outage and recovery time

entries.snippet: How frequently backup copies of essential business information and software are taken;

entries.snippet: The distance of recovery facilities to the CA's main site; and

entries.title: Incident Response and Disaster Recovery Plans
entries.snippet: Procedures for securing its facility to the extent possible during the period of time following a disaster and prior to restoring a secure environment either at the original or a remote site.

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: CA organizations MUST have a mass revocation plan, and as of 2025-12-01, they SHALL assert in section 5.7.1 of their CPS or combined CP/CPS that they maintain a comprehensive and actionable plan for m

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: The CA's mass revocation plan MUST include clearly defined, actionable, and comprehensive procedures designed to ensure rapid, consistent, and reliable response to large-scale certificate revocation s

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Mass revocation provisions MUST include:

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Activation criteria - specific, objective, and measurable thresholds at which the mass revocation plan is triggered based on the CA's risk profile, issuance volumes, and operational capabilities;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Customer contact information - how subscriber and customer contact details are stored, maintained, and kept up to date;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Automation points - processes that are automated or could be automated, and those processes that require manual intervention;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Targets and timelines - for incident triage, revocation initiation, certificate replacement, and post-event review;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Subscriber notification methods - mechanisms for notifying impacted Subscribers;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Role assignments - roles and responsibilities of personnel responsible for initiating, coordinating, and executing the plan;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Training and education - training, awareness, and readiness activities for personnel responsible for, or supporting, the plan;

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Plan testing - annual operational testing to assess readiness and demonstrate implementation feasibility, using one or more of tabletop exercises, simulations, parallel testing, or controlled test env

entries.title: Mass Revocation Plans
entries.snippet: Post-test analysis and update schedule - how lessons learned from testing or live incidents are incorporated into the plan, and how often it is reviewed and updated.

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: For other CA Key Pairs that are for the operator of the Root CA or an Affiliate of the Root CA, the CA SHOULD:

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: have a Qualified Auditor witness the CA Key Pair generation process or record a video of the entire CA Key Pair generation process.

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: In all cases, the CA SHALL:

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: generate the CA Key Pair in a physically secured environment as described in the CA's Certificate Policy and/or Certification Practice Statement;

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: generate the CA Key Pair using personnel in Trusted Roles under the principles of multiple person control and split knowledge;

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: generate the CA Key Pair within cryptographic modules meeting the applicable technical and business requirements as disclosed in the CA's Certificate Policy and/or Certification Practice Statement;

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: log its CA Key Pair generation activities; and

entries.title: CA Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: maintain effective controls to provide reasonable assurance that the Private Key was generated and protected in conformance with the procedures described in its Certificate Policy and/or Certification

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL reject a certificate request if one or more of the following conditions are met:

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: The Key Pair does not meet the requirements set forth in [Section 6.1.5](#615-key-sizes) and/or [Section 6.1.6](#616-public-key-parameters-generation-and-quality-checking);

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: There is clear evidence that the specific method used to generate the Private Key was flawed;

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: The CA is aware of a demonstrated or proven method that exposes the Applicant's Private Key to compromise;

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: The Public Key corresponds to an industry-demonstrated weak Private Key. At least the following precautions SHALL be implemented:

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: In the case of Debian weak keys vulnerability (<https://wiki.debian.org/SSLkeys>), the CA SHALL reject all keys found at <https://github.com/cabforum/Debian-weak-keys/> for each key type (e.g. RSA, EC

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: In the case of ROCA vulnerability, the CA SHALL reject keys identified by the tools available at <https://github.com/crocs-muni/roca> or equivalent.

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: In the case of Close Primes vulnerability (<https://fermatattack.secvuln.info/>), the CA SHALL reject weak keys which can be factored within 100 rounds using Fermat's factorization method.

entries.title: Subscriber Key Pair Generation
entries.snippet: If the Subscriber Certificate will contain an `extKeyUsage` extension containing either the values `id-kp-serverAuth` [RF 5280](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280) or `anyExtendedKeyUsage`

entries.snippet: For RSA key pairs the CA SHALL:

entries.title: Key sizes
entries.snippet: For ECDSA key pairs, the CA SHALL:

entries.title: Key sizes

entries.title: Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Extensions
entries.snippet: In addition to the above, `extKeyUsage` extension requirements vary based on the relationship between the Issuer and Subject organizations represented in the Cross-Certificate.

entries.title: Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Extensions
entries.snippet: The `extKeyUsage` extension MAY be "unrestricted" as described in the following table if:

entries.title: Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Extensions
entries.snippet: the `organizationName` represented in the Issuer and Subject names of the corresponding certificate are either:

entries.title: Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Extensions
entries.snippet: the same, or

entries.title: Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Extensions
entries.snippet: the `organizationName` represented in the Subject name is an affiliate of the `organizationName` represented in the Issuer name

entries.title: Cross-Certified Subordinate CA Extensions
entries.snippet: the corresponding CA represented by the Subject of the Cross-Certificate is operated by the same organization as the Issuing CA or an Affiliate of the Issuing CA organization.

entries.snippet: **Note**: Although each Subscriber Certificate type varies in Subject Information, all Certificates provide the same level of assurance of the device identity (domain name and/or IP address).

entries.title: SubjectPublicKeyInfo

entries.title: SubjectPublicKeyInfo
entries.snippet: The following requirements apply to the `subjectPublicKeyInfo` field within a Certificate or Precertificate. No other encodings are permitted.

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: RSA

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL indicate an RSA key using the `rsaEncryption` (OID: 1.2.840.113549.1.1.1) algorithm identifier. The parameters MUST be present, and MUST be an explicit NULL. The CA SHALL NOT use a differ

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: When encoded, the `AlgorithmIdentifier` for RSA keys MUST be byte-for-byte identical with the following hex-encoded bytes: `300d06092a864886f70d0101010500`

entries.title: ECDSA
entries.snippet: ECDSA

entries.title: ECDSA
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL indicate an ECDSA key using the `id-ecPublicKey` (OID: 1.2.840.10045.2.1) algorithm identifier. The parameters MUST use the `namedCurve` encoding.

entries.title: ECDSA
entries.snippet: For P-256 keys, the `namedCurve` MUST be `secp256r1` (OID: 1.2.840.10045.3.1.7).

entries.title: ECDSA
entries.snippet: For P-384 keys, the `namedCurve` MUST be `secp384r1` (OID: 1.3.132.0.34).

entries.title: ECDSA
entries.snippet: For P-521 keys, the `namedCurve` MUST be `secp521r1` (OID: 1.3.132.0.35).

entries.title: ECDSA
entries.snippet: When encoded, the `AlgorithmIdentifier` for ECDSA keys MUST be byte-for-byte identical with the following hex-encoded bytes:

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier
entries.snippet: All objects signed by a CA Private Key MUST conform to these requirements on the use of the `AlgorithmIdentifier` or `AlgorithmIdentifier`-derived type in the context of signatures.

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier
entries.snippet: In particular, it applies to all of the following objects and fields:

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier
entries.snippet: The `signature` field of a TBSCertList

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier
entries.snippet: The `signatureAlgorithm` field of a BasicOCSPResponse.

entries.title: Signature AlgorithmIdentifier
entries.snippet: No other encodings are permitted for these fields.

entries.title: RSA

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The CA SHALL use one of the following signature algorithms and encodings. When encoded, the `AlgorithmIdentifier` MUST be byte-for-byte identical with the specified hex-encoded bytes.

entries.title: RSA

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: Until 2026-09-15, the CA MAY use the following signature algorithm and encoding if all of the following conditions are met:

entries.title: RSA

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The new Certificate is a Root CA Certificate or Subordinate CA Certificate that is a Cross-Certificate; and,

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: There is an existing Certificate, issued by the same issuing CA Certificate, using the following encoding for the signature algorithm; and,

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The existing Certificate has a `serialNumber` that is at least 64-bits long; and,

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The only differences between the new Certificate and existing Certificate are one of the following:

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: A new `subjectPublicKey` within the `subjectPublicKeyInfo`, using the same algorithm and key size; and/or,

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: A new `serialNumber`, of the same encoded length as the existing Certificate; and/or

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The new Certificate's `extKeyUsage` extension is present, has at least one key purpose specified, and none of the key purposes specified are the `id-kp-serverAuth` (OID: 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.1) or the `any

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The new Certificate's `basicConstraints` extension has a pathLenConstraint that is zero.

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: If used within an OCSP response, such as the `signatureAlgorithm` of a BasicOCSPResponse:

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The `producedAt` field value of the ResponseData MUST be earlier than 2022-06-01 00:00:00 UTC; and,

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: All unexpired, un-revoked Certificates that contain the Public Key of the CA Key Pair and that have the same Subject Name MUST also contain an `extKeyUsage` extension with the only key usage present b

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: If used within a CRL, such as the `signatureAlgorithm` field of a CertificateList or the `signature` field of a TBSCertList:

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The CRL is referenced by one or more Root CA or Subordinate CA Certificates; and,

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: The Root CA or Subordinate CA Certificate has issued one or more Certificates using the following encoding for the signature algorithm.

entries.title: RSA
entries.snippet: **Note**: The above requirements do not permit a CA to sign a Precertificate with this encoding.

entries.title: RSA

entries.title: RSA

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: If the CA asserts compliance with these Baseline Requirements, all CRLs that it issues MUST comply with the following CRL profile, which incorporates, and is derived from [RFC 5280](https://datatracke

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: A full and complete CRL is a CRL whose scope includes all Certificates issued by the CA.

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: A partitioned CRL (sometimes referred to as a "sharded CRL") is a CRL with a constrained scope, such as all Certificates issued by the CA during a certain period of time ("temporal sharding"). Aside f

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: Minimally, CAs MUST issue either a "full and complete" CRL or a set of "partitioned" CRLs which cover the complete set of Certificates issued by the CA within 7 days of such CA issuing its first certi

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: CAs MUST NOT issue indirect CRLs (i.e., the issuer of the CRL is not the issuer of all Certificates that are included in the scope of the CRL).

entries.title: CRL Issuing Distribution Point

entries.title: CRL Issuing Distribution Point
entries.snippet: The `onlySomeReasons` field SHOULD NOT be included; if included, then the CA MUST provide another CRL whose scope encompasses all revocations regardless of reason code.

entries.title: Delayed Revocation
entries.snippet: demonstrated the implementation and feasibility of the plans through testing exercises, including documentation of testing processes, timelines, results, and remediation steps; and

entries.title: Delayed Revocation
entries.snippet: incorporated feedback from such testing exercises and other evaluations to enhance readiness and improve future performance.

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: Google Chrome relies on Certification Authority systems (herein referred to as "CAs") to issue certificates to websites. Chrome uses these certificates to help ensure the connections it makes on behal

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: When making HTTPS connections, Chrome refers to a list of self-signed root certificates from CAs that have demonstrated why continued trust in them is justified. This list is known as a "Root Store."

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: The Chrome Root Program Policy below establishes the minimum requirements for CA certificates to be included as trusted in a default installation of Chrome.

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: The Chrome Root Program continuously evolves this policy to enhance the security and resilience of the Internet ecosystem, consistent with Google's ongoing [commitment](#chromes-ongoing-commitment-to-

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: Except in the case of CA Owners applying for inclusion, this policy makes no stipulation on the characteristics or PKI use cases served by CAs not included in the current version of the Chrome Root St

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: Any questions regarding this policy can be directed to chrome-root-program [at] google [dot] com.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: This policy considers a "CA Owner" to be the organization or legal entity that is either:

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: This policy considers an "Applicant" to be an organization or legal entity that has an open "Root Inclusion Request" submitted to Google Chrome in the [CCADB](https://ccadb.org/).

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate Lifecycle Management
entries.snippet: Subordinate CA Certificate Lifecycle Management

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate Lifecycle Management
entries.snippet: The lifecycle management of subordinate CA certificates plays a crucial role in promoting agility and resilience within the ecosystem. By setting clear expectations for their validity and renewal, the

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate Lifecycle Management
entries.snippet: To achieve these goals, it's encouraged that all subordinate CA certificates that validate to a certificate included in the Chrome Root Store align with the following practices:

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate Lifecycle Management
entries.snippet: Subordinate CA certificates SHOULD have a maximum validity period of three (3) years.

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate Lifecycle Management
entries.snippet: CA Owners SHOULD create and deploy new subordinate CA certificates at least once every year. CA Owners SHOULD actively transition subscriber certificate issuance to these newly deployed CAs.

entries.title: Promote use of Dedicated TLS Server Authentication PKI Hierarchies
entries.snippet: The Chrome Root Store is solely relied upon for TLS server authentication in Chrome; it is not used for any other PKI use case (e.g., TLS client authentication, secure email, code-signing, etc.).

entries.title: Promote use of Dedicated TLS Server Authentication PKI Hierarchies

entries.title: Promote use of Dedicated TLS Server Authentication PKI Hierarchies

entries.snippet: if disclosed to the CCADB **on or after June 15, 2026**: include the extendedKeyUsage extension and only assert an extendedKeyUsage purpose of id-kp-serverAuth.

entries.title: Promote use of Dedicated TLS Server Authentication PKI Hierarchies
entries.snippet: NOT contain a public key corresponding to any other unexpired or unrevoked certificate that asserts different extendedKeyUsage values.

entries.title: Promote use of Dedicated TLS Server Authentication PKI Hierarchies
entries.snippet: All corresponding subscriber certificates issued **on or after March 15, 2027**, MUST include the extendedKeyUsage extension and only assert an extendedKeyUsage purpose of id-kp-serverAuth.

entries.title: S/MIME

entries.title: Program Participation Requirements
entries.snippet: Program Participation Requirements

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: General Obligations

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.1.** Commercial CAs may not enroll a root CA into the Program that is intended to be primarily trusted internally within an organization (i.e. Enterprise CAs).

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.2.** Program Participants must provide Microsoft the identities and emails of at least two "Trusted Agents" to serve as representatives to the Program and one general email alias. Program Partic

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.3.** At least one of the contacts or aliases provided should be a 24/7 monitored communications channel for revocation requests or other incident management situations.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.4.** The Program Participant must disclose its full PKI hierarchy (nonlimited subordinate CA, cross-signed nonenrolled root CAs, subordinate CAs, EKUs, certificate constraints) to Microsoft on a

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.5.** If a subordinate CA isn't publicly disclosed or audited, it must be domain-constrained.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.6.** Program Participants must inform Microsoft via email at least 120 days before transferring ownership of enrolled root or subordinate CA that chains to an enrolled root to another entity or

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.7.** Reason Code must be included in revocations for intermediate certificates. CAs must update the CCADB when revoking any intermediate certificates within 30 days.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.8.** If a CA uses a subcontractor to operate any aspect of its business, the CA will assume responsibility for the subcontractor's business operations.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.9.** Program Participants must provide to Microsoft evidence of a Qualifying Audit for each root, unconstrained subordinate CA, and cross-signed certificate before conducting commercial operatio

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.10.** Program Participants must assume responsibility to ensure that all unconstrained subordinate CAs and cross-signed certificates meet the Program Audit Requirements.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.11.** CAs must publicly disclose all audit reports for unconstrained subordinate CAs.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.12.** Program Participants agree that Microsoft may contact customers that Microsoft believes may be substantially impacted by the pending removal of a root CA from the Program.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.13.** If Microsoft, in its sole discretion, identifies a certificate whose usage or attributes are determined to be contrary to the objectives of the Trusted Root Program or the Baseline Require

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.14.**  CAs trusted by Microsoft products MUST comply with the most recent and applicable CA/B Forum Guidelines/Baseline Requirements (BRs) for the type of certificate they issue, as defined by t

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.15.**  No single organization, including Microsoft, has the authority to grant exceptions to the Baseline Requirements. Microsoft will not grant exceptions under any circumstances.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.16.**  TRP Participants MUST adhere to the latest version of the CCADB Policy.

entries.title: General Obligations
entries.snippet: **2.1.17.** Certificate Authorities MUST update their Certificate Policy (CP) and Certification Practice Statement (CPS) documents before applying any change in operations. The updated documents must

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: Root Requirements

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.1.** Root certificates must be x.509 v3 certificates.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.2.** Certificates to be added to the Trusted Root Store MUST be self-signed root certificates.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.3.** The CN attribute must identify the publisher and must be unique.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.4.** The CN attribute must be in a language that is appropriate for the CA's market and readable by a typical customer in that market.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.5.** Basic Constraints extension: must be cA=true.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.6.** Key Usage extension MUST be present and MUST be marked critical. Bit positions for KeyCertSign and cRLSign MUST be set. If the Root CA Private Key is used for signing OCSP responses, then t

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.7.** Root Key Sizes must meet the requirements detailed in "Signature Requirements" below.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.8.** Newly minted Root CAs must be valid for a minimum of eight years, and a maximum of 25 years, from the date of submission.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.9.** Participating Root CAs may not issue new 1024-bit RSA certificates from roots covered by these requirements.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.10.** All issuing CA certificates must contain either a CDP extension with a valid CRL and/or an AIA extension to an OCSP responder. An end-entity certificate may contain either an AIA extension

entries.snippet: **3.1.16.** CAs may not have more than 2 OIDs applied to their root certificate.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.17.** End-entity certificates that include a Basic Constraints extension in accordance with IETF RFC 5280 must have the cA field set to FALSE and the pathLenConstraint field must be absent.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.18.** A CA must technically constrain an OCSP responder such that the only EKU allowed is OCSP Signing.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: **3.1.19.** A CA must be able to revoke a certificate to a specific date as requested by Microsoft.

entries.title: Root Requirements
entries.snippet: | ECC / ECDSA | NIST P-256, P-384, P-521 | Not Supported |

entries.snippet: Microsoft requires all Government CAs that submit an EA to provide an attestation letter from the auditor that:

entries.snippet: Lists the Government CA's government's criteria for auditor qualification, and certifies that the auditor meets this criteria.

entries.title: Content of the Equivalent Audit Report
entries.snippet: Lists the particular statutes, rules, and/or regulations that the auditor assessed the Government CAs operations against.

entries.title: Content of the Equivalent Audit Report
entries.snippet: Certifies the Government CA's compliance with the requirements outlined in the named governing statutes, rules, and/or regulations.

entries.title: Content of the Equivalent Audit Report
entries.snippet: Provides information that describes how the statute's requirements are equivalent to the appropriate WebTrust or ETSI audits.

entries.title: Content of the Equivalent Audit Report
entries.snippet: Lists Certificate Authorities and third parties authorized by the Government CA to issue certificates on the Government CA's behalf within a certificate chain.

entries.title: Content of the Equivalent Audit Report
entries.snippet: Documents the full PKI hierarchy.

entries.title: Content of the Equivalent Audit Report
entries.snippet: Provides the start and end date of the audit period.

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: Several Public Root Store Operators have collaborated to create the Common Certification Authority Database (CCADB), a data repository of certificate and Certification Authority (CA) information.

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: CA Owners who wish to be included in a CCADB-participating Root Store ("Root Store") need to disclose and maintain certain information in the CCADB. This policy describes common Root Store Operator re

entries.title: Introduction
entries.snippet: Root Store Operators MAY have additional CCADB-related requirements defined in their own Root Store policies. Those policies MAY strengthen the requirements described in this policy. Additional inform

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this policy are to be interpreted as described in [RFC 2119](https:/

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: This policy considers a "CA Owner" to be the organization or legal entity that is either:

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: represented in the subject DN of the CA certificate; or

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: in possession or control of the corresponding private key capable of issuing new certificates, if not the same organization or legal entity directly represented in the subject DN of the certificate.

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: General Provisions

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: When required by a Root Store Operator policy, CA Owners MUST adhere to the current version of this policy. Failure to adhere to this policy MAY result in a Root Store Operator disabling a CA Owner’s

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: Regardless of more specific provisions in these requirements, CA Owners have an overarching responsibility to keep the information in the CCADB about themselves, their operations and their certificate

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: All CCADB disclosures MUST be made freely available and without additional requirements, including, but not limited to, registration, legal agreements, or restrictions on redistribution of the certifi

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: All data in the CCADB may be made public or semi-public in a variety of forms. CA Owners SHOULD NOT place any information in the CCADB which they wish to keep confidential.

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: Root Store Operators are not obliged to publish any CCADB information.

entries.title: General Provisions
entries.snippet: All client devices that are used to download Personally Identifiable Information from the CCADB SHOULD employ disk-based encryption.

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: Contact Information Disclosures

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST provide the following information for a Primary Point of Contact (POC), and at least one other POC, who MAY be a Primary POC:

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: Name

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owner-affiliated email address

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MAY optionally designate further POCs, supplying an email address for each. CA Owners MUST also supply at least one non-personal contact email alias that is more likely to continue working a

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: All Primary POCs SHOULD be authorized to speak for and enter into binding commitments on behalf of the CA(s) that they represent. The Primary POC will be issued a [CA Community license](https://www.cc

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: Notification of security and audit-related issues MAY be emailed to all individual POCs, non-personal contact email alias(es), or a combination of these addresses. CA Owners are advised to make sure t

entries.title: Contact Information Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST maintain up to date contact details in the CCADB. If POC information needs to be updated, the CA Owner MUST submit an [Add/Update Contacts Case](https://www.ccadb.org/cas/contacts). A C

entries.title: Root CA Certificates
entries.snippet: Root CA Certificates

entries.title: Root CA Certificates
entries.snippet: A root CA certificate is a self-signed certificate issued by the root CA to identify itself and to facilitate verification of certificates issued by its corresponding PKI hierarchy.

entries.title: Root CA Certificates
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST maintain correct and current information about their root CA certificates. If root CA certificate information needs to be added or updated, the CA Owner MUST submit an [Add/Update Root

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: Subordinate CA Certificates

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: A subordinate CA certificate is a certificate signed by a root CA or another subordinate CA. The CCADB considers the terms "intermediate" and "subordinate" synonymous.

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST disclose:

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: all subordinate CA certificates capable of validating to a certificate included in a Root Store or associated with a CCADB Root Inclusion Request. Disclosure MUST take place within 7 calendar days of

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: revocation of all subordinate CA certificates capable of validating to a certificate included in a Root Store or associated with a CCADB Root Inclusion Request within 7 calendar days of revocation.

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: Cross-certificates (i.e., where the same subject and public key of an existing CA certificate appears in at least one additional certificate issued by a _different_ CA Owner) are considered subordinat

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: Ultimately, this means the subject CA certificate may appear in two distinct CA Owner PKI hierarchies, but it MUST minimally appear in the issuing CA Owner’s.

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificates
entries.snippet: For any revoked subordinate CA certificate, each corresponding revocation entry published to a CRL MUST include a reasonCode extension. If the revocation is due to a security concern, the CA Owner MUS

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: Policy Disclosures

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: CCADB enables associating PKI policy documents in the records for both root CA certificates and subordinate CA certificates.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners with either (1) a certificate included in a Root Store or (2) a CA certificate that validates to a certificate included in a Root Store MUST minimally:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: accurately describe the policies and practices of their CA(s) within a Certificate Policy (CP) and corresponding Certification Practice Statement (CPS), or preferably, a single document combined as a

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: ensure the CA policy documents are:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: freely publicly available for examination.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: available in an authoritative English language version.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: sufficiently detailed to assess the operations of the CA(s) and the compliance with the expectations set forth in this Policy, the applicable CA/Browser Forum Baseline Requirements, and any applicable

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: To promote simplicity and clarity, all CA policy documents SHOULD be:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: focused on one specific PKI use case (e.g., TLS server authentication, TLS client authentication, S/MIME, Code Signing, etc.), rather than combining multiple use cases into a single document or set of

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: comprehensive and consolidated, whenever possible, such that there are not multiple sets of similar yet slightly different policy and practice statements supporting the same PKI use case.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: available in Markdown or AsciiDoc.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST strictly adhere to their policy document(s) as disclosed within the CCADB (and not marked as “Superseded”). This extends to all policy documents the CA Owner publishes in relation to it

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST ensure that updated versions of a CA's policy document(s) are uploaded to their own publicly accessible repository before the corresponding policy changes are put into practice. Corresp

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: submit an [Add/Update Root Request Case](https://www.ccadb.org/cas/updates) to add or update policy documents for root CA certificates stored in the CCADB.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: add or update  policy documents for subordinate CA certificates directly on the record in CCADB (unless the exceptions stated below apply).

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: Unless the exceptions below apply, CA policy disclosures for each certificate disclosed to the CCADB MUST be consistent with the following guidance:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: If the parent certificate record discloses a CP or CPS:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: A) It MUST disclose both a CP and a CPS

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: B) It MUST NOT disclose a CP/CPS

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: C) All child certificate records MUST either:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: (1) disclose a combined CP/CPS; or

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: (2) disclose (a) a CP (either select "Same as Parent" checkbox or a different CP) and (b) a CPS (either select "Same as Parent" checkbox or a different CPS).

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: If the parent certificate record discloses a combined CP/CPS:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: A) It MUST NOT disclose a CP

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: B) It MUST NOT disclose a CPS

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: C) All child certificate records MUST either:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: (1) disclose the same combined CP/CPS (using the "Same as Parent" checkbox);

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: (2) disclose a different CP/CPS; or

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: (3) disclose a CP and a CPS.

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: Exceptions to providing these policy document URLs:

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: The certificate has expired; or

entries.title: Policy Disclosures
entries.snippet: The certificate has been revoked, and the corresponding record in the CCADB has been updated with the correct revocation status.

entries.snippet: Audit Disclosures

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: Beyond policy disclosures, CCADB also enables associating statements of attestation of CA Owner conformance to various requirements and other operational criteria ("audits").

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The URLs to such audit statements and any metadata about them such as the name of the auditor or the date of the audit MUST be updated as new information becomes available. For technical reasons, URLs

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The entry for each subordinate CA certificate has checkboxes to indicate "Audits Same as Parent". When the checkbox is checked, the details do not need to be duplicated from the parent certificate. Ho

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: URLs for the following audit statements are required for each CA certificate, depending upon the purposes for which the certificate and those it issues are trusted.

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: Some Root Store Operators require additional audit disclosures to the CCADB.

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: Exceptions to providing these audit statement URLs:

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate is specifically listed as in scope in the audit statements of the parent certificate, and the "Audits Same as Parent" checkbox is checked; or

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The certificate has expired; or

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The certificate has been revoked, and the corresponding record in the CCADB has been updated with the correct revocation status; or

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The certificate was issued after the audit period of the most recently performed audit. In such cases, the certificate MUST be included in the scope of the CA Owner's next periodic audit.

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: CA Owners MUST:

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: submit an [Add/Update Root Request Case](https://www.ccadb.org/cas/updates) to add or update audit statements for root CA certificates stored in the CCADB.

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: add or update audit statements for subordinate CA certificates directly on the record in the CCADB (unless the exceptions stated above apply).

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: If an audit or audit statement disclosed to the CCADB does not meet the requirements of this policy, then a Root Store Operator MAY require that the CA Owner obtain a new audit, at the CA Owner's expe

entries.title: Audit Disclosures
entries.snippet: The presence of qualifications in an audit statement is not, by itself, generally considered a reason to remove a CA Owner from a Root Store. The purpose of audits is to honestly and thoroughly assess

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Audit Statement Content

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: An authoritative English language version of publicly available audit information MUST be uploaded to the CCADB no later than 92 calendar days from the point-in-time date or the end date of the period

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Reports uploaded to the CCADB MUST be publicly available and contain at least the following clearly-labeled text-searchable information:

entries.title: Audit Statement Content

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: The physical locations that were and were not audited;

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: SHA-256 fingerprint of each root and subordinate CA certificate that was in scope of the audit (see format specifications below);

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Full names and version numbers of the audit standards that were used during the audit;

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: List of the CA Owner's applicable policy documents (with version numbers and publication dates) referenced during the audit;

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Whether the audit is for a period of time or a point in time;

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Start date and end date of the period that was audited, for those that cover a period of time (this is not the period the auditor was on-site);

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Point-in-time date, for those that are for a point in time;

entries.title: Audit Statement Content
entries.snippet: Date the audit statement was issued (referred to as the "issuing date" by the ACAB'c Audit Attestation Letter templates and "report date" by the WebTrust Illustrative Reports);
