INTRODUCTION
entries.snippet: INTRODUCTION

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: Overview

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: This combined Certificate Policy ("CP") and Certification Practice Statement ("CPS") document ("CP/CPS") outlines the certification services practices for Internet Security Research Group ("ISRG") Pub

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: ISRG PKI services include, but are not limited to, issuing, managing, validating, and revoking publicly-trusted Certificates in a manner consistent with this CP/CPS.

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: These services are provided to the general public with exceptions as ISRG management deems appropriate or in accordance with relevant law.

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: ISRG PKI services are most commonly, but not necessarily exclusively, provided under the brand/trademark "Let's Encrypt".

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: The ISRG PKI conforms to the current version of the Baseline Requirements for the Issuance and Management of Publicly-Trusted Certificates published at <https://www.cabforum.org>. In the event of any

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: Other documents related to the behavior and control of the ISRG PKI, such as a Subscriber Agreement and Privacy Policy, can be found in the Policy and Legal Repository.

entries.title: Overview
entries.snippet: Per IETF PKIX RFC 3647, this CP/CPS is divided into nine components that cover security controls, practices, and procedures for certification services provided by the ISRG PKI.

entries.title: Document name and identification

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: This is the ISRG CP/CPS. This document was approved for publication by the ISRG Policy Management Authority, and is made available in the Policy and Legal Repository.

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: The following revisions have been made:

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | May 5, 2023 | CP and CPS documents are combined. See past documents for prior version history. | 5.0 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | May 16, 2023 | Make CPS OID and URL in Subscriber Certificates optional. | 5.1 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Feb 7, 2024 | Add CRL Distribution Point to subscriber certificate profile. Add IssuingDistributionPoint to subordinate CRL profile. Remove id-qt-cps from subscriber certificate profile, make it opt

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Mar 22, 2024 | Improve accuracy of information regarding subscriber key compromise in sections 4.9.3 and 4.9.12. | 5.3 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Sep 27, 2024 | Remove statements regarding meaningful names. Format section headers to match RFC 3647. | 5.4 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Oct 25, 2024 | Consolidate statements about certificate contents into Section 7.1. Replace normative language with descriptive language. | 5.5 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Dec 12, 2024 | Make OCSP service optional. | 5.6 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Jan 15, 2025 | Update trusted roles, make TLS Client Auth optional, document IP Address validation. | 5.7 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Jul 30, 2025 | Updates regarding MPIC, Short-lived Certificate revocation, Root CA Certificate profile, WebTrust audits, and HSM validation standards. Minor language consistency and clarity updates

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Aug 29, 2025 | Add mass revocation readiness language. | 5.9 |

entries.title: Document name and identification
entries.snippet: | Nov 5, 2025 | Add ISRG Root YE and Root YR. | 6.0 |

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **ACME Protocol**: A protocol described by [RFC 8555](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8555) and used for validation, issuance, and management of certificates. The protocol is an open standard manage

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **CAB Forum**: Certificate Authority / Browser Forum, a group of CAs and browsers which come together to discuss technical and policy issues related to PKI systems. (<https://cabforum.org/>)

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Certificate Repository**: A repository of information about ISRG certificates. It is located at: <https://letsencrypt.org/certificates/>

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Identifier**: A value included, or requested to be included, in a Subscriber Certificate's Subject Common Name field or Subject Alternative Name extension, such as a Fully-Qualified Domain Name (FQD

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Policy and Legal Repository**: A repository of policy and legal documents related to the ISRG PKI. It is located at: <https://letsencrypt.org/repository/>

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Precertificate**: As defined by RFC 6962 Section 3.1.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Secure PKI Facilities**: Facilities designed to protect sensitive PKI infrastructure, including CA private keys.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.snippet: **Trusted Contributor**: A contributor who performs in a Trusted Role. Trusted Contributors may be employees, contractors, or community members. Trusted Contributors must be properly trained and quali

entries.title: Definitions

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: Prior to issuance of a Subscriber Certificate, ISRG uses at least one of the following methods to validate the Applicant's control of each requested DNS name:

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: DNS Change (Baseline Requirements Section 3.2.2.4.7)

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: Agreed-Upon Change to Website - ACME (Baseline Requirements Section 3.2.2.4.19)

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: TLS Using ALPN (Baseline Requirements Section 3.2.2.4.20)

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: Similarly, ISRG uses at least one of the following methods to validate the Applicant's control of each requested IP address:

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: ACME "http-01" method for IP Addresses (Baseline Requirements Section 3.2.2.5.6)

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: ACME "tls-alpn-01" method for IP Addresses (Baseline Requirements Section 3.2.2.5.7)

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: Validation for Wildcard Domain Names is only performed using the DNS Change method.

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity
entries.snippet: All successful validations and CAA checks performed by our Primary Network Perspectives are corroborated by multiple remote Network Perspectives located in at least two distinct Regional Internet Regi

entries.title: Authentication of organization identity

entries.title: Circumstances for revocation
entries.snippet: ISRG revokes certificates in accordance with Section 4.9.1.1 and Section 4.9.1.2 of the Baseline Requirements.

entries.title: Circumstances for revocation
entries.snippet: Depending on the circumstances, revocation timelines can be as short as 24 hours or even less. Therefore, ISRG strongly recommends against using publicly-trusted TLS server certificates on systems tha

entries.title: Who can request revocation
entries.snippet: Who can request revocation

entries.title: Who can request revocation
entries.snippet: Anyone can request revocation via the certificate revocation interface of the ACME Protocol, as defined in Section 7.6 of RFC 8555.

entries.title: Who can request revocation
entries.snippet: Requests for revocation may also be made by emailing [cert-prob-reports@letsencrypt.org](mailto:cert-prob-reports@letsencrypt.org).

entries.title: Who can request revocation
entries.snippet: ISRG may administratively revoke certificates if it determines that the Subscriber has failed to meet obligations under this CP/CPS, the relevant Subscriber Agreement, or any other applicable agreemen

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: Procedure for revocation request

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: Revocation requests made via the ACME API are processed automatically. The certificate identified in the revocation request is revoked if:

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: the request is signed with the private key associated with the certificate;

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: the request is signed with the account key of the Subscriber which originally requested issuance of the certificate; or

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: the request is signed with the account key of a Subscriber who has demonstrated control over all Subject Alternative Names in the certificate.

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: ISRG maintains a continuous (24x7) ability to accept and respond to revocation requests and Certificate Problem Reports. ISRG responds to such requests within 24 hours, though an investigation into th

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: An investigation into whether revocation or other appropriate action is warranted is based on at least the following criteria:

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: The nature of the alleged problem;

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: The number of Certificate Problem Reports received about a particular Certificate or Subscriber;

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: The entity making the complaint; and

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: Relevant legislation.

entries.title: Procedure for revocation request
entries.snippet: In all cases, requests to revoke Short-lived Subscriber Certificates may be ignored.

entries.title: Revocation request grace period
entries.snippet: Revocation request grace period

entries.title: Revocation request grace period
entries.snippet: There is no grace period for a revocation request. A revocation request must be made as soon as reasonably possible after circumstances requiring revocation are confirmed.

entries.title: Time within which CA must process the revocation request
entries.snippet: Time within which CA must process the revocation request

entries.title: Time within which CA must process the revocation request
entries.snippet: Investigation into a revocation request begins within 24 hours of receiving the request. Revocation, if necessary, is carried out within the timeframes set by Sections 4.9.1.1 and 4.9.1.2 of the Basel

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: Root CA Certificate

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Serial Number                  | Unique, with 64 bits of output from a CSPRNG                            |

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Issuer Distinguished Name      | C=US, O=Internet Security Research Group or O=ISRG, and a meaningful CN |

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Distinguished Name     | Same as Issuer DN                                                       |

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Validity Period                | Up to 25 years                                                          |

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Basic Constraints              | cA=True, pathLength constraint absent (critical)                        |

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Public Key             | See Sections 6.1.5, 6.1.6, and 7.1.3.1                                  |

entries.title: Root CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Key Usage                      | keyCertSign, cRLSign (critical)                                         |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: Subordinate CA Certificate

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Serial Number                  | Unique, with 64 bits of output from a CSPRNG                                  |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Issuer Distinguished Name      | Derived from Issuer certificate                                               |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Distinguished Name     | C=US, O=Let's Encrypt, and a meaningful CN                                    |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Validity Period                | Up to 8 years                                                                 |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Basic Constraints              | cA=True, pathLength constraint 0 (critical)                                   |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Key Usage                      | keyCertSign, cRLSign, digitalSignature (critical)                             |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Extended Key Usage             | TLS Server Authentication and optionally TLS Client Authentication            |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Certificate Policies           | CAB Forum Domain Validated (2.23.140.1.2.1)                                   |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Authority Information Access   | Contains CA Issuers URL and optionally an OCSP URL; URLs vary based on Issuer |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Public Key             | See Sections 6.1.5, 6.1.6, and 7.1.3.1                                        |

entries.title: Subordinate CA Certificate
entries.snippet: | CRL Distribution Points        | Contains a CRL URL; URL varies based on Issuer                                |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Serial Number                     | Unique, with 64 bits of output from a CSPRNG                                      |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Issuer Distinguished Name         | Derived from Issuer certificate                                                   |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Distinguished Name        | CN=none, or one of the values from the Subject Alternative Name extension         |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Validity Period                   | Up to 100 days                                                                    |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Basic Constraints                 | cA=False (critical)                                                               |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Key Usage                         | digitalSignature, and optionally keyEncipherment (critical)                       |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Extended Key Usage                | TLS Server Authentication and optionally TLS Client Authentication                |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Certificate Policies              | CAB Forum Domain Validated (2.23.140.1.2.1)                                       |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Authority Information Access      | Contains CA Issuers URL and optionally an OCSP URL; URLs vary based on Issuer     |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Public Key                | See Sections 6.1.5, 6.1.6, and 7.1.3.1                                            |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Subject Alternative Name          | A sequence of 1 to 100 dNSNames or ipAddresses (critical if no CN)                |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Precertificate poison             | Per RFC 6962 (precertificates only, critical)                                     |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | Signed Certificate Timestamp List | Per RFC 6962 (final certificates only)                                            |

entries.title: DV-SSL Subscriber Certificate
entries.snippet: | CRL Distribution Point            | If present, contains a URI to the CRL shard whose scope includes this certificate |

entries.snippet: For the status of Subordinate CA Certificates:

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | Version                   | V2                                                                             |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | Signature Algorithm       | sha256WithRSAEncryption or ecdsa-with-SHA384                                   |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | ThisUpdate                | The date and time when the Certificate revocation list validity begins         |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | NextUpdate                | Up to ThisUpdate + 1 year                                                      |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | RevokedCertificates       | Contains: userCertificate, revocationDate, reasonCode                          |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | CRLnumber                 | The serial number of this CRL in an incrementally increasing sequence of CRLs  |

entries.snippet: | IssuingDistributionPoint  | If present, asserts onlyContainsCACerts                                        |

entries.title: CRL profile

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | ThisUpdate                | The date and time when the Certificate revocation list validity begins                       |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | NextUpdate                | Up to ThisUpdate + 10 days                                                                   |

entries.title: CRL profile
entries.snippet: | RevokedCertificates       | Contains: userCertificate, revocationDate, reasonCode                                        |

entries.title: CRL profile

entries.title: CRL profile

entries.title: Notification mechanism and period
entries.snippet: ISRG posts CP/CPS revisions to its Repository. ISRG does not guarantee or set a notice-and-comment period and may make changes to this CP/CPS without notice.
